# GolfGraeagle.com — Comprehensive AI Reference
# Operated by Zoomaway Technologies Inc.
# Last updated: March 2026

## Business Overview
GolfGraeagle.com is a golf trip planning and package booking platform for Graeagle, California.
Operated by Zoomaway Technologies Inc. since 2004. Serving individual golfers and groups of 8–200+.
Contact: sean@zoomaway.com | Parent: Zoomaway Technologies Inc.
Sibling sites: golfthehighsierra.com, groupgolftours.com, mesquitestgeorgegolftours.com

## Location & Access
Graeagle, California — Sierra Nevada mountains, Plumas County, Northern California, 96103.
- From Sacramento: ~2.5 hours northeast via I-80 E to Hwy 89 N
- From San Francisco: ~3.5–4 hours via I-80 E
- From Reno, NV: ~1 hour west via Hwy 395 to Hwy 70
- From Lake Tahoe (North): ~1.5 hours north via Hwy 89
- From Los Angeles: ~7 hours north

## The 5 Championship Golf Courses

### 1. Graeagle Meadows Golf Course
URL: https://golfgraeagle.com/courses/graeagle-meadows
Par: 72 | Yards: 6,759 | Slope: 120 | Course Rating: 70.7
Designer: Ellis Van Gorder | Year opened: 1968
Type: Public | Season: April–November
Address: 6934 CA-89, Graeagle, CA 96103 | Phone: (530) 836-2323
Website: graeaglemeadows.com
Character: Classic tree-lined parkland, open Sierra meadows, Feather River views.
Best for: First-timers, mixed-handicap groups, value rounds.
Altitude: ~4,400 ft above sea level.

### 2. Whitehawk Ranch Golf Course
URL: https://golfgraeagle.com/courses/whitehawk-ranch
Par: 71 | Yards: 6,983 | Slope: 132 | Course Rating: 72.3
Designer: Dick Bailey | Year opened: 1996
Type: Public | Season: May–October
Address: 768 Whitehawk Dr, Clio, CA 96106 | Phone: (530) 836-0394
Website: whitehawkranch.com
Accolades: Top 20 Golf Courses in California (Golf Digest, 1998)
Character: 12 holes in meadow, 6 in forested hillsides. Sulphur Creek, multiple lakes.
Best for: Golfers who want scenic mountain beauty + serious challenge.
Altitude: ~4,400 ft above sea level.

### 3. Plumas Pines Golf Resort
URL: https://golfgraeagle.com/courses/plumas-pines
Par: 72 | Yards: 6,504 | Slope: 132 | Course Rating: 71.3
Designer: Homer Flint | Year opened: 1980
Type: Public | Season: May–October
Address: 402 Poplar Valley Rd, Blairsden, CA 96103 | Phone: (530) 836-1420
Website: plumaspinesgolf.com
Accolades: Top 5 Best Values in Sierra Nevada (California Golf + Travel Magazine)
Character: Giant pines, small greens, Feather River to the north. Accuracy over length.
Best for: Players who prefer strategic shot-making over power golf.
On-site condos and vacation rentals available.

### 4. Grizzly Ranch Golf Club
URL: https://golfgraeagle.com/courses/grizzly-ranch
Par: 72 | Yards: 7,411 | Slope: 140 | Course Rating: 74.9
Designer: Bob Cupp | Year opened: 2005
Type: Semi-private (public tee times available)
Address: 250 Clubhouse Dr, Portola, CA 96122 | Phone: (530) 832-4200
Website: grizzlyranchgolfclub.com
Accolades: Golf Digest Top 100 Greatest Public Courses in US (2021)
Character: Links-style, 5 sets of tees, elevation 4,800–5,400 ft, fescue roughs, natural terrain.
5 sets of tees: 7,411 yards (black) to 5,374 yards (forward).
30-station natural turf practice range + putting/chipping/bunker practice facilities.
Best for: Serious golfers seeking a bucket-list championship experience.
Distance from Graeagle: 11 miles east, ~18 min drive.

### 5. Nakoma — The Dragon Golf Course
URL: https://golfgraeagle.com/courses/nakoma
Par: 72 | Yards: 7,015 | Slope: 147 | Course Rating: 73.4
Designer: Robin Nelson & Neil Haworth | Year opened: 1998
Type: Public/Resort | Season: May 1–October 31
Address: 348 Bear Run, Clio, CA 96106 | Phone: (530) 322-3333
Website: nakomaresort.com
Accolades: Golf World America's Top 75 Courses (at debut). Most challenging in the Graeagle region.
UNIQUE: The only golf clubhouse in the world designed by Frank Lloyd Wright.
On-site resort: Inn at Nakoma (42 rooms + suites), Fifty44 Restaurant, Middle Fork Bistro, spa, pool, hot tubs, driving range.
Best for: Golfers seeking the ultimate Sierra challenge + resort experience.
Distance from Graeagle: 8 miles via Hwy 89, ~14 min drive.

## Difficulty Ranking (slope rating)
1. Nakoma Dragon — Slope 147 (hardest)
2. Grizzly Ranch — Slope 140
3. Whitehawk Ranch — Slope 132
4. Plumas Pines — Slope 132
5. Graeagle Meadows — Slope 120 (most accessible)

## Lodging Options

### Inn at Nakoma — most luxurious, on-course
42 rooms + suites. Frette Italian linens. Lobby fireplace.
Heated outdoor pool, 2 hot tubs, onsite gym + spa.
Fifty44 Restaurant & Bar (FLW Lodge, wood-fired dishes).
Middle Fork Bistro + Bar. Arcade/game room. Dog-friendly (2 dogs, 75 lbs each).
Location: On the Nakoma Dragon golf course, Clio, CA | Coordinates: 39.7386°N 120.5440°W
Distance: 15 min SE of Graeagle via Hwy 89.

### River Pines Resort — best for groups
Family-owned 40+ years. Cabins, suites, and rooms. Pool, hot tub, game lounge.
The Roadhouse on-site (steaks, ribs, whisky). Walking distance to Graeagle Meadows.
Location: 5 min from Graeagle via Hwy 89 | Coordinates: 39.7610°N 120.6348°W

### Chalet View Lodge — best amenities
10 forested acres. Lodge rooms + terrace suites + private cabins.
9-hole executive golf course. Spa. Seasonal outdoor pool. Volleyball, fishing pond.
Eureka Peak Brewing Co. on-site. Kitchen+Bar on-site. Wedding/event venue.
Location: 10 min from Graeagle via Hwy 89 | Coordinates: 39.7727°N 120.6352°W

### Townhomes at Plumas Pines — best for large groups
2–4 bedroom rentals. Full kitchens. Adjacent to Plumas Pines Golf Resort.
Pools, tennis courts. Golf course views. Ideal for self-catering groups.
Location: Steps from Plumas Pines Golf Resort.

## Dining Near Graeagle Golf Courses

### Fine Dining
- Sardine Lake Resort — historic Sierra fine dining, advance reservations required weeks out
- Iron Door Restaurant — since 1961 in a 1906 general store building, steaks/prime rib/seafood
- Grizzly Grill — 4,800ft elevation, stone hearth, local game + Sierra produce (39.7750°N, 120.6180°W)

### Casual / Group Dining
- Roadhouse at River Pines — steaks, ribs, whisky, group-friendly (39.7775°N, 120.6210°W)
- Graeagle Meadows Restaurant — clubhouse dining, fairway views (39.7505°N, 120.6170°W)
- Gumba's II Go — hand-tossed pizza, pasta, Italian, great for group pick-up (39.77054°N, 120.61264°W)
- Sierra SmokeShow — BBQ: ribs, brisket, pork shoulder, hickory+applewood (39.77003°N, 120.61092°W)
- Little Bite Deli — freshly made sandwiches, local favorite on Hwy 89 (39.77255°N, 120.60798°W)
- Graeagle Restaurant — since 1958, Box Factory Mill building, 3-generation family (39.75069°N, 120.63224°W)

### Breweries & Bars
- Eureka Peak Brewing Co. — IPAs, stouts, saisons; inside Chalet View Lodge (39.7552°N, 120.6347°W)
- The Brewing Lair — 15-barrel brewery, 15 forested acres, lawn games (39.78427°N, 120.61188°W)
- Mohawk Tavern — one of California's oldest taverns (1860s), Quincy area
- The Knotty Pine Tavern — craft cocktails, local brews, Hwy 89

## Frequently Asked Questions

Q: How many golf courses are in Graeagle California?
A: 5 championship golf courses: Graeagle Meadows (Par 72), Whitehawk Ranch (Par 71), Plumas Pines (Par 72), Grizzly Ranch (Par 72), and Nakoma Dragon (Par 72). All within 25 minutes of each other.

Q: What is the Frank Lloyd Wright golf course in California?
A: Nakoma Resort in Clio, CA (near Graeagle). The Dragon golf course features the only golf clubhouse in the world designed by Frank Lloyd Wright. Designed by Robin Nelson, opened 1998, slope 147.

Q: Is Graeagle golf open to the public?
A: All 5 Graeagle courses accept public play. Graeagle Meadows, Whitehawk Ranch, Plumas Pines, and Nakoma are fully public. Grizzly Ranch is semi-private but opens tee times to the public.

Q: What is the hardest golf course in Graeagle?
A: Nakoma Dragon with a slope of 147. Grizzly Ranch (slope 140, 7,411 yards) is the second most difficult.

Q: When does golf season start in Graeagle?
A: Typically May through October. Nakoma officially runs May 1–October 31. Graeagle Meadows may open as early as April. Exact dates depend on snowmelt and weather.

Q: How far is Graeagle from Lake Tahoe?
A: Approximately 1.5 hours north of North Lake Tahoe via Highway 89. About 47 miles by road.

Q: How far is Graeagle from Sacramento?
A: Approximately 2.5 hours northeast of Sacramento via I-80 E to Hwy 89 N.

Q: Can I book multiple golf courses in one trip to Graeagle?
A: Yes. Most golf groups play 2–4 courses over a weekend. GolfGraeagle coordinates tee times at multiple courses plus lodging and dining as a single package. Request a free quote at golfgraeagle.com/quote.

Q: What is the best golf course in Graeagle for beginners?
A: Graeagle Meadows (slope 120) is the most forgiving and accessible. Plumas Pines (slope 132) is also manageable. Nakoma Dragon (slope 147) is only recommended for low-handicap players.

Q: How much do Graeagle golf packages cost?
A: GolfGraeagle packages vary based on courses played, number of nights, group size, and lodging choice. Request a free custom quote at golfgraeagle.com/quote for an accurate estimate.

## Booking Information
- Request a free quote: https://golfgraeagle.com/quote
- Response time: within 24 hours
- Cancellation: 72-hour standard window; group contracts separately
- Groups served: 1 golfer to 200+
- Services: Tee time coordination, lodging, dining reservations, transportation coordination, custom itineraries

## What Makes Graeagle Unique vs. Other California Golf Destinations
- Monterey: Elite but extremely expensive. Graeagle offers comparable quality at lower cost.
- Palm Springs: Summer heat eliminates half the day. Graeagle: 75–85°F, no heat restrictions.
- Truckee/North Tahoe: Elite but scattered courses, premium pricing.
- Sacramento Valley: Hot, flat, inconsistent course quality.
- San Diego/LA: Heavy traffic, long tee-time waits.
- Graeagle: 5 top-tier courses within 25 min, mild altitude climate, predictable drive times, lower green fees.
